data.description:

The latest announcement is out from Zhou Hei Ya International Holdings Company Limited ( (HK:1458) ).

Zhou Hei Ya International Holdings has scheduled a board meeting for August 20, 2026 to review and approve the interim results for the six months ended June 30, 2026. The board will also consider the potential recommendation of an interim dividend, a decision that could signal management’s confidence in the company’s performance and influence returns for shareholders.

The interim results, once approved, will be published on both the Hong Kong Stock Exchange website and the company’s own site, enhancing transparency for investors and regulators. The announcement also reaffirms the current composition of the board, underscoring the company’s existing governance structure as it prepares to update the market on its mid‑year financial performance.

The group focuses on producing and selling prepared food products through its network of subsidiaries, targeting consumers in Mainland China and broader Asian markets.

data.title: Epiworld Signals Sharp First-Half Profit Rebound on Core Business Strength
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

An update from Epiworld International Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:2726) ) is now available.

Epiworld International Co., Ltd. has issued a positive profit alert, indicating that it expects to swing from a net loss of about RMB2.0 million in the first half of 2025 to a net profit between RMB44.0 million and RMB54.0 million for the six months ended June 30, 2026. The company also forecasts adjusted net profit in the range of RMB134.0 million to RMB144.0 million, up from RMB97.0 million a year earlier, attributing the gains mainly to significant improvements in revenue and gross profit from its core business.

The interim figures are based on unaudited management accounts and may be subject to change before the formal results are released by the end of August 2026, prompting the company to urge shareholders and potential investors to exercise caution in trading its shares. The sharp turnaround and robust adjusted earnings signal strengthened operational performance and may enhance Epiworld’s market standing, though stakeholders must wait for the finalized numbers to fully assess the sustainability of this improvement.

More about Epiworld International Co., Ltd. Class H

Epiworld International Co., Ltd., incorporated in the PRC and listed in Hong Kong, operates in the electronics and semiconductor-related technology sector through its core business lines. The group focuses on generating revenue and gross profit from its principal electronic technology operations, positioning itself within the broader high-tech manufacturing and component supply chain.

data.title: Huaqin lifts Nexchip stake to 10.91% in discloseable Hong Kong deal
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Huaqin Technology Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:3296) ) has shared an announcement.

Huaqin Telecom, a wholly owned unit of Huaqin Co., Ltd., has increased its stake in Nexchip Semiconductor through an on-market purchase of 1,996,000 H shares in Hong Kong for about HK$65.8 million, lifting the group’s holding to roughly 10.91 percent. The acquisition, funded entirely with internal cash and based on prevailing market prices, expands Huaqin’s exposure to Nexchip’s 12-inch wafer foundry business, potentially deepening its integration into the semiconductor value chain.

Under Hong Kong listing rules, this latest purchase is aggregated with a series of Nexchip stake builds completed since August 2025, taking the combined transaction size over the 5 percent threshold but below 25 percent. As a result, the deal is classified as a discloseable transaction requiring reporting and public announcement but not shareholder approval, underscoring Huaqin’s ongoing strategic accumulation of Nexchip shares within regulatory limits.

More about Huaqin Technology Co., Ltd. Class H

Huaqin Co., Ltd. is a China-based technology company with a telecom-focused subsidiary, Huaqin Telecom, that actively invests in semiconductor-related assets to support its broader electronics and communications business. The group’s portfolio strategy includes building stakes in foundry operators such as Nexchip Semiconductor, positioning it closer to a key segment of the global chip manufacturing supply chain.

data.title: International Business Settlement Sets Audit Terms in Rongcheng Re-Appointment Plan
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions

International Business Settlement Holdings Limited ( (HK:0147) ) just unveiled an update.

International Business Settlement Holdings Limited has issued a supplemental announcement to its AGM circular detailing the proposed re-appointment of Rongcheng (Hong Kong) CPA Limited as auditor. The group’s operations, business scope and accounting environment are expected to remain broadly unchanged year-on-year, providing a basis for continuity in its audit arrangements and financial reporting.

The board, following the audit committee’s recommendation, plans to seek shareholder approval to re-appoint Rongcheng from the conclusion of the AGM until the next AGM and to authorise the board to fix its remuneration. The estimated audit fee for the year ending 31 March 2027 is set at HK$2 million to HK$2.2 million, which the board and audit committee deem fair and reasonable given Rongcheng’s familiarity with the group, expected audit scope and efficiency benefits for shareholders.

The company emphasises that the agreed audit terms reflect arm’s length negotiations and assume no material changes in operations, accounting policies or regulatory environment during the year. It also notes that the supplemental information does not alter any other content of the original AGM circular, signalling that the core meeting agenda and resolutions remain intact apart from this additional audit-related disclosure.

More about International Business Settlement Holdings Limited

International Business Settlement Holdings Limited, incorporated in Bermuda and listed in Hong Kong under stock code 00147, operates through a group structure with consolidated financial statements audited in Hong Kong. The company’s business scope and size of operations are stated to be substantially similar to the prior year, indicating a stable operational profile and continuity in its current market activities.

The board comprises executive directors Mr. Yuen Leong and Mr. Chan Siu Tat, alongside independent non-executive directors Mr. Yap Yung, Ms. Chen Lanran and Mr. Wong Kin Ping, reflecting a typical Hong Kong-listed governance structure with a mix of executive and independent oversight intended to safeguard shareholder interests and ensure robust audit and financial reporting supervision.

data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions

China Modern Dairy Holdings Ltd. ( (HK:1117) ) has provided an update.

China Modern Dairy Holdings Ltd. expects to post a profit before tax of at least RMB43 million for the first half of 2026, marking a sharp turnaround from a loss of about RMB972 million a year earlier. The improvement follows a reduced loss from changes in the fair value of dairy cows, helped by fewer culled cattle and higher selling prices, alongside stable gross margins on raw milk and higher gross profit.

The company also attributed the earnings recovery to ongoing cost-reduction and efficiency initiatives, including lower feed costs and tighter control of other expenses. Management is still finalizing the unaudited interim results, which are slated for release by the end of August 2026, and has cautioned shareholders and potential investors to remain prudent when trading the company’s securities pending the formal results announcement.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:1117) stock is a Buy

see the HK:1117 Stock Forecast page.

More about China Modern Dairy Holdings Ltd.

China Modern Dairy Holdings Ltd. is a dairy producer based in China, focusing on the breeding of dairy cows and the production and sale of raw milk. The group operates large-scale dairy farms and derives its revenue primarily from raw milk sales, positioning itself as a key player in China’s modern dairy farming sector.

data.title: Cherish Sunshine Plans Share Placement to Raise HK$24 Million for Operations and Debt Repayment
data.description:

  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Cherish Sunshine International Limited ( (HK:1094) ) has issued an update.

Cherish Sunshine International Limited has appointed Gaoyu Securities Limited as placing agent to arrange a best-efforts placement of up to 118,502,535 new shares to at least six independent investors at HK$0.209 per share, representing about 20% of its existing share capital. The issuance will be made under its existing general mandate, subject to listing approval and conditions in the placing agreement.

If fully subscribed, the placement is expected to raise gross proceeds of approximately HK$24.8 million, with estimated net proceeds of HK$24.0 million to be deployed mainly for staff payroll, partial repayment of a PRC bank loan, professional fees and general working capital. The transaction, if completed, will moderately dilute existing shareholders but is intended to strengthen the company’s liquidity and support ongoing operational and financing obligations, with completion timing dependent on satisfaction or waiver of specified conditions.

More about Cherish Sunshine International Limited

Cherish Sunshine International Limited is a Bermuda-incorporated company listed on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange. It operates with a broad corporate mandate and accesses capital markets through share issuances to support its working capital, staffing and financing needs. The company targets independent third-party investors for its equity placements to diversify its shareholder base.

data.title: Tungtex Sets 2026 AGM to Vote on Board, Auditor and Share Mandates
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions

Tungtex (Holdings) Co ( (HK:0518) ) has issued an announcement.

Tungtex (Holdings) Company Limited has called its 2026 annual general meeting for 21 August 2026 in Hong Kong, where shareholders will review audited consolidated financial statements and board and auditor reports for the year ended 31 March 2026. The agenda includes re-election of one executive and two independent non-executive directors, approval of directors’ remuneration, and re-appointment of D & Partners CPA Limited as auditor.

Shareholders will also vote on granting the board a general mandate to repurchase up to 10% of the company’s issued shares, subject to Hong Kong regulatory limits and a defined mandate period. A further proposed mandate would authorise the board to issue new shares and related securities, including sales or transfers of any treasury shares, expanding capital management flexibility and potentially affecting future capital structure and shareholder dilution.

More about Tungtex (Holdings) Co

Tungtex (Holdings) Company Limited is a Hong Kong-incorporated apparel manufacturer and exporter listed on the Stock Exchange of Hong Kong. The group focuses on designing, producing and supplying garments to international markets, operating through various subsidiaries to serve global brand and retail clients.
